name: Trillo - Cifuentes – circular por el Alto Tajo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Trillo features routes through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, characterized by river gorges, pine forests, and varied elevations. The region offers a mix of terrain, from riverside paths to more challenging climbs. Trails often traverse areas with views of the Tagus River and its reservoirs, providing diverse landscapes for mountain bikers. Best mountain bike trails around Trillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Trillo - Embalse de Entrepeñas – circular por el Alto Tajo, a difficult 29.7 miles (47.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 37 minutes to complete, offering extensive views over the reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Trillo - Cifuentes – circular por el Alto Tajo, a moderate 21.4 miles (34.4 km) path. This route explores the natural beauty of the Alto Tajo, passing through woodlands and open areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Hundido de Armallones – circular desde Arbeteta por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a 31.2 miles (50.2 km) trail leading through the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Trillo is defined by river valleys, pine forests, and reservoir views. The network offers options for moderate to advanced ability levels. The routes in Trillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Trillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Trillo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Trillo primarily cater to moderate and difficult skill levels. There are 16 moderate routes and 5 difficult routes available, offering a good challenge for experienced riders. There are no easy routes listed in the area.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Trillo?
A: While there are no routes specifically categorized as 'easy' for beginners or young children, some of the moderate trails might be suitable for families with older, more experienced riders. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain before heading out.

Q: What are some of the longest mountain bike trails near Trillo?
A: For a longer ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Durón – View of Alocén loop from Durón&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 50 km and is rated as difficult. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004696261/hundido-de-armallones-circular-desde-arbeteta-por-el-parque-natural-del-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundido de Armallones – circular desde Arbeteta por el Parque Natural del Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 50.2 km trail.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer scenic viewpoints of the reservoirs or rivers?
A: Yes, many trails in the Trillo area offer stunning views, particularly of the Tagus River and its reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003290673/trillo-embalse-de-entrepenas-circular-por-el-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillo - Embalse de Entrepeñas – circular por el Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, provides extensive views over the reservoir.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Trillo?
A: Absolutely. Many of the mountain bike trails around Trillo are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004339955/trillo-cifuentes-circular-por-el-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillo - Cifuentes – circular por el Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17507866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Tajo – Cifuentes River Waterfall loop from Trillo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I see while mountain biking around Trillo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, being part of the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You can encounter river gorges, pine forests, and the Tagus River. Notable natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5902743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5369072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area around Trillo features several historical sites. You might pass by settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5610565" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arbeteta&lt;/a&gt;, and even catch views of historical structures such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Arbeteta Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7249960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Ocentejo Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Trillo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Trillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 5 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful river valleys, and the extensive views over the reservoirs.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Trillo?
A: Given the region's characteristics, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Trillo, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, and winters might bring colder weather, though many trails remain accessible year-round.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for advanced riders?
A: For advanced riders seeking a challenge, Trillo offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003290673/trillo-embalse-de-entrepenas-circular-por-el-alto-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trillo - Embalse de Entrepeñas – circular por el Alto Tajo&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 47.7 km trail with significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38174798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Durón – View of Alocén loop from Durón&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 50 km.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails around Trillo?
A: The terrain around Trillo is diverse, ranging from riverside paths along the Tagus River to more challenging climbs through pine forests and open areas within the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces and elevations, characteristic of a natural park environment.
tips: The quiet town of Trillo is surrounded by a beautiful landscape environment and is crossed by the Cifuentes River, which in its passage through the urban area has a series of very picturesque waterfalls that are the most visited site.
Walk around the 14th century fortress, built by Don Juan Manuel. The castle has five towers, dominates the landscape and dominates the village below.
